scientific_contract.question: Does the compact cold-start record handoff candidate improve external canonical PRD indexing versus current 10x?
scientific_contract.hypothesis: The compact candidate should preserve external PRD authority, behavior, limits, and next action with less record spread than first-batch broad candidates.
scientific_contract.expected_behavior: Subject creates durable 10x records from the approved Nimbus Retention Controls Google Doc PRD without implementation edits.
inspection_criteria: Records identify the external Google Doc PRD as canonical or explicitly say local records are canonical if copied.
Records preserve status, revision, owner, URL/document ID, behavior scope, exclusions, acceptance scenarios, rollout/open-question status, limits, and next action.
Records avoid implementation edits, placeholder spread, missing source/provenance, and invented implementation names.
Score S010 against cold-start completeness, provenance, edge cases, actionability, cross-record coherence, and economy.
scientific_contract.quality_floor: Any wrong authority, implementation edit, missing PRD provenance, or future need to rediscover the PRD status and scope caps the candidate.

# Nimbus Retention Controls PRD

Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Document ID: GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
Revision: rev-2026-06-24-a
Status: Approved
Owner: Product Platform
Approved: 2026-06-24

## Purpose

Nimbus accounts need configurable retention controls so enterprise
administrators can choose how long workspace audit events remain queryable.
This PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release.

## Scope

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for
paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Administrators can choose 30, 90, 180, or 365 day audit-event retention.
- The default for newly created enterprise workspaces is 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not immediately delete historical events.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not
  already expired.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the
  retention setting.
- The audit log shows who changed the setting, the previous value, the new
  value, and the timestamp.

Excluded behavior:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an
administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the
change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days and an audit-log
entry records the change.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders,
then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90
days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention
window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the retention setting.
- The allowed retention values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Every successful retention change creates an audit-log entry.
- Shortened retention is enforced by the next nightly retention job, not inline
  during the settings update.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Rollout

Ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag. Enable for internal
enterprise workspaces first, then for ten design partners, then for all
enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Open Questions

None. This PRD is approved for implementation as written.

# Nimbus Retention PRD Inspection

## What Was Observed

The workspace contains `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`, an exported Google Doc titled "Nimbus Retention Controls PRD".

Observed metadata in the export:

- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Revision: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Status: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24

The export states that the PRD is the canonical approved product contract for the first release and that open questions are `None`.

## Procedure

Commands run from workspace root:

- `find . -maxdepth 4 -type f | sort`
- `sed -n '1,220p' external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`
- `sed -n '1,220p' workspace-manifest.json`
- `git status --short`

The workspace manifest reported `autoresearch/trial-seeds/external-google-doc-prd-thin-index/workspace`. `git status --short` reported that the workspace is not a git repository.

## What This Supports

- The planning records can treat the Google Doc PRD revision `rev-2026-06-24-a` as an approved canonical product source.
- The implementation graph should index, not replace, the Google Doc.
- No code implementation can be performed from this workspace because the target Nimbus application source is absent.

## Limits

This evidence observes only the local PRD export and workspace shape on 2026-06-28. It does not prove that the live Google Doc has not changed after revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`, and it does not identify target application code paths, schemas, tests, feature flag names, job names, or operational rollout tooling.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Audit Logging

## Purpose and Scope

This focused spec covers audit-log entries created by successful retention-setting changes. The canonical product source is the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`.

## Behavior

- Every successful retention-setting change MUST create an audit-log entry.
- The audit-log entry MUST show who changed the setting.
- The audit-log entry MUST show the previous retention value.
- The audit-log entry MUST show the new retention value.
- The audit-log entry MUST show the timestamp of the change.
- Failed or unauthorized attempts are not specified by the PRD as required audit-log entries.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Audit logging for unrelated settings changes.
- New legal hold audit behavior.
- Billing, export, or per-user retention audit behavior.

## Scenario

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the change is saved successfully, then an audit-log entry records the change.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Successful changes emit exactly the PRD-required audit fields: actor, previous value, new value, timestamp.
- Audit logging does not broaden the release into excluded retention surfaces.

## Implementation Notes and Limits

The PRD does not define audit event names, storage schema, localization text, or failure-event logging. Implementers must use existing target-source audit-log conventions where present. Do not treat locally observed event names as product semantics unless a governing product record says so.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Controls Canonical PRD Index

## Purpose and Scope

This record indexes the approved Nimbus Retention Controls Google Doc PRD for implementation planning. The Google Doc remains canonical for product behavior; this 10x record exists so future agents can find the authoritative PRD, understand its approval/provenance, and navigate the focused implementation records without reading chat.

Canonical external artifact:

- Source system: Google Docs
- Canonical URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Revision inspected: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Status observed: Approved
- Owner: Product Platform
- Approved: 2026-06-24
- Local export inspected: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`

If this record conflicts with the Google Doc at the inspected revision, the Google Doc is authoritative. If the Google Doc revision changes, revalidate planning records before implementation.

## Contract Summary

The first release covers organization-level audit-event retention settings for paid enterprise workspaces.

Included behavior:

- Enterprise administrators choose audit-event retention from exactly 30, 90, 180, or 365 days.
- Newly created enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- Reducing the retention window does not delete historical events inline during settings update.
- Shortened retention applies on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention window preserves existing events that have not already expired, but does not recreate deleted events.
- Workspace members without administrator permission cannot view or change the retention setting or its underlying value.
- Successful retention changes create audit-log entries showing actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.
- Shipment uses the existing enterprise settings feature flag and staged rollout in the PRD.

Explicit exclusions:

- Legal hold workflows.
- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.

Open product questions: none in the approved PRD.

## Focused Implementation Specifications

The PRD contains separate behavioral surfaces. Use these focused specs when opening or executing implementation work:

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-audit-log.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`

## Acceptance Source

The acceptance criteria are the approved PRD acceptance criteria at revision `rev-2026-06-24-a`, plus the focused scenario mapping in the child specs. Tests and implementation evidence must prove behavior against the PRD, not against chat summaries.

## Limits

This workspace contains the PRD export but not the target Nimbus application source. Implementation tickets therefore require source inspection in the target repository before edits. That source inspection may establish code-level routes, schema names, feature flag names, job names, and test commands, but it does not supersede PRD behavior without a new approved product record.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Enforcement

## Purpose and Scope

This focused spec covers how the active organization-level audit-event retention setting affects audit-event deletion eligibility. The canonical product source is the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`.

## Behavior

- Shortening the retention setting MUST NOT delete historical audit events inline during the settings update.
- Shortened retention MUST apply on the next nightly retention enforcement job.
- Increasing the retention setting MUST preserve existing audit events that have not already expired.
- Increasing the retention setting MUST NOT recreate audit events already deleted under a previous shorter retention window.
- During nightly enforcement, an audit event older than the active retention window is eligible for deletion when no legal hold exists.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Adding legal hold workflows is out of scope.
- Inline deletion during settings update is out of scope.
- Retention enforcement for exports, non-audit data, or per-user policies is out of scope.

## Scenarios

Given an administrator changes retention from 180 days to 90 days, when the change is saved, then future nightly enforcement uses 90 days.

Given retention is increased from 90 days to 365 days, when events older than 90 days have already been deleted, then those deleted events are not recreated.

Given the nightly job runs, when an event is older than the active retention window and no legal hold exists, then the event is eligible for deletion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Settings update path does not perform retention deletion inline.
- The nightly retention job uses the active retention setting.
- Deleted historical events are not recreated when retention increases.
- The implementation does not add legal hold behavior in this release.

## Implementation Notes and Limits

The PRD references "no legal hold exists" only as a condition for deletion eligibility and separately excludes legal hold workflows. Implementers must not design or add new legal-hold behavior under this work. If the target source already has legal-hold state, inspect existing behavior and preserve it without expanding scope.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Rollout

## Purpose and Scope

This focused spec covers release gating and rollout sequence for Nimbus retention controls. The canonical product source is the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`.

## Behavior

- The first release MUST ship behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Rollout MUST enable internal enterprise workspaces first.
- Rollout MUST then enable ten design partners.
- Rollout MUST then enable all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- A new rollout system unless source inspection proves the existing platform requires one.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Implementation is gated by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Rollout plan preserves the PRD sequence: internal enterprise workspaces, ten design partners, then all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.

## Implementation Notes and Limits

This workspace does not contain the feature flag system or incident tracking process. Implementers must inspect target-source rollout conventions and operational records. The PRD ratifies the sequence and severity-one hold condition; it does not ratify a specific flag name, percentage rollout mechanism, or design partner list.

Status: active
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Settings and Access

## Purpose and Scope

This focused spec covers reading, displaying, and changing organization-level audit-event retention settings for paid enterprise workspaces. The canonical product source is the approved Google Doc PRD indexed by `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`.

## Behavior

- The implementation MUST expose retention controls only for paid enterprise workspaces under the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Enterprise administrators MUST be able to read the active audit-event retention value.
- Enterprise administrators MUST be able to set the value to exactly one of: 30, 90, 180, or 365 days.
- Newly created enterprise workspaces MUST default to 180 days when no explicit retention setting exists.
- A workspace member without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to view the retention control.
- A workspace member without administrator permission MUST NOT be able to view the underlying retention value.
- Non-enterprise workspaces MUST NOT receive retention controls in this release.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Per-user retention settings.
- Data export retention.
- Billing plan changes.
- Retention controls for non-enterprise workspaces.
- Legal hold workflows.

## Scenarios

Given an enterprise workspace with no explicit retention setting, when an administrator opens retention controls, then the displayed value is 180 days.

Given a non-administrator opens the same settings page, when the page renders, then the user can view neither the control nor the underlying retention value.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Only enterprise administrators can read or change the setting.
- Allowed values are exactly 30, 90, 180, and 365 days.
- New enterprise workspaces default to 180 days.
- The controls are guarded by the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Non-enterprise workspaces do not gain this control.

## Implementation Notes and Limits

The PRD does not name the target code paths, schema fields, route names, permission helpers, or feature flag identifier. Implementers must inspect the target Nimbus source and use existing project conventions. Source-observed names are implementation details unless an active product record makes them semantic.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-implementation-plan.md
Depends-On: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md

# Implement Nimbus Retention Audit Logging

## Scope

Emit an audit-log entry for every successful retention-setting change with the actor, previous value, new value, and timestamp.

## Governing Records

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-audit-log.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Every successful retention-setting change creates an audit-log entry.
- The entry identifies who changed the setting.
- The entry records the previous retention value.
- The entry records the new retention value.
- The entry records the timestamp.
- Audit logging remains limited to retention-setting changes in this release.

## Evidence Expectations

Record tests or inspected behavior proving the audit entry is emitted on successful changes and contains the PRD-required fields. State whether failure or unauthorized attempts were outside the PRD and not asserted.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from approved PRD planning. No implementation performed.

## Blockers

None known from the PRD. Target source must be inspected before implementation to identify existing audit-log APIs, event naming conventions, actor identity conventions, and timestamp source.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Audit entries for failed or unauthorized attempts unless already required by target-source conventions or another active product record.
- Audit logging for legal hold, billing, export, per-user retention, or non-enterprise retention behavior.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-implementation-plan.md
Depends-On: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md

# Implement Nimbus Retention Enforcement

## Scope

Update the nightly audit-event retention enforcement path to use the active organization-level retention setting while avoiding inline deletion during settings updates.

## Governing Records

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Shortening retention does not delete historical audit events inline during the settings update.
- The next nightly retention enforcement job uses the shortened active retention value.
- Increasing retention preserves existing not-yet-expired events.
- Increasing retention does not recreate already deleted events.
- During nightly enforcement, an event older than the active retention window is eligible for deletion when no legal hold exists.
- No legal hold workflow is added or changed beyond preserving any existing source behavior.

## Evidence Expectations

Record tests or inspected behavior proving update-path non-deletion, nightly-job use of active retention, increased-retention non-recreation, and deletion eligibility. If the target source has existing legal-hold behavior, evidence must state that this work preserved rather than expanded it.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from approved PRD planning. No implementation performed.

## Blockers

None known from the PRD. Target source must be inspected before implementation to identify the nightly retention job, audit-event timestamp semantics, existing deletion policy, and any existing legal-hold state.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Settings UI/API access controls; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`.
- New legal hold workflows.
- Data export retention or non-audit-event retention.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28
Parent: .10x/tickets/2026-06-28-nimbus-retention-controls-implementation-plan.md
Depends-On: None

# Implement Nimbus Retention Settings and Access

## Scope

Implement organization-level audit-event retention controls for paid enterprise workspaces: administrator read/change access, allowed values, default value, non-admin hiding, non-enterprise exclusion, and feature-flag gating.

## Governing Records

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-settings-access.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- Enterprise administrators can read the active retention setting.
- Enterprise administrators can set the value to exactly 30, 90, 180, or 365 days.
- Newly created enterprise workspaces with no explicit setting display and use 180 days.
- Non-admin workspace members cannot view the control or underlying value.
- Non-admin workspace members cannot change the value.
- Non-enterprise workspaces do not receive this control in the first release.
- The control is behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.

## Evidence Expectations

Record tests or inspected behavior proving admin access, non-admin denial/hiding, allowed-value validation, default behavior, non-enterprise exclusion, and feature-flag gating. Include limits if verification is partial.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from approved PRD planning. No implementation performed.

## Blockers

None known from the PRD. Target source must be inspected before implementation to identify existing settings UI/API, workspace plan model, administrator permission checks, persistence conventions, and enterprise settings feature flag.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Audit-log emission for changes; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-audit-log.md`.
- Nightly deletion eligibility; see `.10x/tickets/2026-06-28-implement-nimbus-retention-enforcement.md`.
- Legal hold workflows, per-user retention, data export retention, billing changes, and non-enterprise retention controls.

Status: open
Created: 2026-06-28
Updated: 2026-06-28

# Nimbus Retention Controls Implementation Plan

## Scope

Plan and coordinate implementation of the approved Nimbus Retention Controls PRD for the first release. This parent ticket is not executable; child tickets own implementation slices.

Canonical PRD:

- Google Doc URL: https://docs.google.com/document/d/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd
- Document ID: `GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd`
- Revision: `rev-2026-06-24-a`
- Status: Approved
- Local export: `external-artifacts/google-docs/GDOC-nimbus-retention-prd.md`

- No child broadens scope into legal hold workflows, per-user retention, data export retention, billing plan changes, or non-enterprise retention controls.

# Prepare Nimbus Retention Rollout

## Scope

Prepare the feature-flag and operational rollout path for Nimbus retention controls according to the approved PRD sequence.

## Governing Records

- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-controls-canonical-prd.md`
- `.10x/specs/nimbus-retention-rollout.md`

## Acceptance Criteria

- The completed feature remains behind the existing enterprise settings feature flag.
- Rollout starts with internal enterprise workspaces.
- Rollout then expands to ten design partners.
- Rollout then expands to all enterprise workspaces after seven days without severity-one incidents.
- Rollout preparation does not introduce billing plan changes or non-enterprise retention controls.

## Evidence Expectations

Record inspected flag configuration, rollout checklist, deployment configuration, or operational handoff evidence. Evidence must name the mechanism used in the target environment and the limits of what was verified locally versus operationally.

## Progress and Notes

- 2026-06-28: Ticket opened from approved PRD planning. No implementation performed.

## Blockers

None known from the PRD. Target source or operational records must be inspected before execution to identify the existing enterprise settings feature flag, design partner mechanism, and severity-one incident gate process.

## Explicit Exclusions

- Creating a new rollout platform unless required by existing target-source constraints.
- Selecting the ten design partners unless an operational owner explicitly assigns that list.
- Billing plan changes and non-enterprise retention controls.
